@@ -219,7 +219,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-eli","title":"Research CASS session data format and API","description":"# Task: Research CASS session data format and API ## Context Part of EPIC: Agent Session Sharing and Replay (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0sb) ## Why This Comes First Before we can export sessions, we need to understand how CASS stores them: - What format is the session data? - What commands does CASS expose? - What fields are available for export? - Are there multiple session types (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini)? ## Research Questions 1. Run 'cass --help' and document available commands 2. Run 'cass list' and examine output format 3. Run 'cass get --json' and examine structure 4. Check if CASS stores sessions per-agent or unified 5. Identify what data is NOT stored (we can't export what doesn't exist) ## Deliverables - Document CASS CLI commands and their output - Example session data structure - List of exportable fields - Any limitations or gaps discovered ## Acceptance Criteria - CASS API documented - Session format understood - Export schema can be designed based on actual data - Gaps identified for follow-up ## Notes This is research, not implementation. Output is documentation that informs subsequent tasks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:13.705596-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:52:01.46799-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:52:01.46799-05:00","close_reason":"Research complete. Documented CASS session format, CLI commands, search/export APIs, session storage locations, and identified 7 limitations. Created docs/cass-session-format-research.md."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-eooz","title":"Add password clarification (VPS root vs provider account)","description":"# Task: Add Password Clarification ## Parent Epic [EPIC] First SSH Connection Experience (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3) ## Description Users frequently confuse their VPS provider account password (Hetzner/Contabo login) with the VPS root password (set during VPS creation). This is the #1 support issue. They try the wrong password, get locked out, and panic. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/ssh-connect/page.tsx Add a PROMINENT AlertCard above the SSH command: \`\`\`tsx

When SSH asks for a password, use the VPS root password — the one you created (or received via email) when setting up the VPS.

❌ NOT this password:

Your Hetzner/Contabo/OVH account login

✓ USE this password:

The root password from VPS creation

Some providers email you the root password. Check your inbox if you didn't set one.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Appears PROMINENTLY, not buried in SimplerGuide - [ ] Clear visual distinction between wrong and right password - [ ] Mentions email option for some providers - [ ] Uses warning styling to grab attention - [ ] Works well on mobile (stacked layout) ## UI/UX Notes - This is SO common that it deserves main-content placement - The two-column comparison makes it instantly clear - Mobile should stack vertically - Consider adding link back to VPS creation step if they forgot","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:41:38.986261-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:19.783445-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:19.783445-05:00","close_reason":"Already covered: ssh-connect/page.tsx has AlertCard + Step 6 explaining VPS password vs hidden typing"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-epc","title":"[codex.1] Fix doctor.sh codex auth detection","description":"## Goal Update scripts/lib/doctor.sh to correctly detect codex-cli authentication via OAuth tokens instead of checking for OPENAI_API_KEY. ## Current (Incorrect) Behavior The check_codex_auth() function currently: 1. Checks for OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable 2. Looks for OPENAI_API_KEY in ~/.zshrc.local 3. Looks for OPENAI_API_KEY in /data/projects/.envrc 4. Reports "pass" if any of these exist ## Correct Behavior Should instead: 1. Check for ~/.codex/auth.json (or similar OAuth token file) 2. Optionally verify the token isn't expired 3. Report "pass" if OAuth credentials exist 4. Suggest running "codex auth" if not authenticated ## Technical Notes - Need to verify the actual path where codex-cli stores OAuth tokens - The token file location may vary by OS/version - Common locations: ~/.codex/, ~/.config/codex/, ~/.local/share/codex/ ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Update check_codex_auth() function ## Acceptance Criteria 1. No mention of OPENAI_API_KEY in codex auth check 2. Correctly detects OAuth authentication 3. Provides helpful guidance for unauthenticated users 4. Regenerate scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh after changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:35.325519-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:37:13.931458-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:37:13.931458-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed check_codex_auth to detect OAuth tokens in ~/.codex/auth.json instead of checking OPENAI_API_KEY env var. Also cleaned up redundant shell redirects.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-epc","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:35.68967-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-epc","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-wy1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:37.126029-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-eqhy","title":"Fresh-eyes review: recent installer/docs changes","description":"Re-review the latest installer+docs changes with fresh eyes; fix any issues (e.g., TARGET_HOME override semantics) and re-run quality gates.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T17:16:46.385048-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T17:16:51.405028-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-eqhy","title":"Fresh-eyes review: recent installer/docs changes","description":"Re-review the latest installer+docs changes with fresh eyes; fix any issues (e.g., TARGET_HOME override semantics) and re-run quality gates.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T17:16:46.385048-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T17:19:38.961316-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T17:19:38.961316-05:00","close_reason":"Fresh-eyes pass complete; fixed TARGET_HOME override semantics (respect when running as TARGET_USER) and added validation; ran bash -n + shellcheck; pushed."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-f5m0","title":"Update manifest generator to include Claude fallback pattern and security fixes","description":"The install_agents.sh file is generated from acfs.manifest.yaml but has been manually customized with: (1) Fallback pattern that tries verified install first, then falls back to direct curl install, (2) Robust associative array check using grep for 'declare -A', (3) Safe variable initialization before array access. These customizations will be LOST if someone runs 'bun run generate'. The generator in packages/manifest/src/generate.ts should be updated to include this pattern so regeneration preserves these improvements.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:40:18.360744-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T02:47:32.095844-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T02:47:32.095844-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed: Updated generator with resilient fallback pattern, added fallback_url field to schema, regenerated install_agents.sh"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fac","title":"EPIC: Project Foundation & Infrastructure","description":"# Epic: Project Foundation & Infrastructure ## Overview Set up the foundational infrastructure for the ACFS project, including repo structure, tooling, and development environment. ## Background & Reasoning Before any feature development can begin, we need a solid foundation: - Proper monorepo structure for website + installer + onboard - Consistent tooling (bun, TypeScript, linting) - CI/CD pipeline for quality gates - Documentation standards ## Success Criteria - [ ] All directory structure matches AGENTS.md spec - [ ] bun workspace configured properly - [ ] CI runs on every PR - [ ] shellcheck passes on all bash scripts ## Dependencies None - this is the foundation ## Considerations - Keep structure minimal initially, add complexity only when needed - Prefer convention over configuration - Make it easy for agents to understand and navigate ## Relationship to Project Goals This enables all subsequent development by providing a consistent, well-documented codebase structure that both humans and AI agents can work with effectively.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:19:59.763401-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:24:12.701537-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:24:12.701537-05:00","close_reason":"Foundation complete: directory structure, install.sh, library scripts, configs all created","labels":["epic","infrastructure"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","title":"EPIC: Module Manifest System","description":"# Epic: Module Manifest System ## Overview Create the module manifest system (acfs.manifest.yaml) that serves as single source of truth for all installed tools. ## Background & Reasoning Without a manifest, we'd have: - Scattered install logic - No way to generate doctor checks automatically - Website and installer out of sync - Hard to add/update tools The manifest provides: - Canonical list of all tools - Install commands - Verify commands - Dependencies between modules - Metadata for website display ## Manifest Structure ```yaml version: 1 name: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup id: acfs defaults: user: ubuntu workspace_root: /data/projects mode: vibe modules: - id: stack.ntm description: Named tmux manager (agent cockpit) category: stack priority: 1 install: - curl -fsSL https://... | bash verify: - ntm --help aliases: - ntm docs_url: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm ``` ## Generated Outputs 1. **Website**: Tools checklist on status page 2. **Installer**: Module installation scripts 3. **Doctor**: Check definitions with IDs 4. **Documentation**: Auto-generated tool reference ## Success Criteria - [ ] Complete manifest covering all 40+ tools - [ ] Parser library in packages/manifest - [ ] Generator for website content - [ ] Generator for installer modules - [ ] Generator for doctor checks - [ ] CI validates manifest syntax ## Considerations - Keep YAML human-readable - Use consistent IDs across the project - Document the schema thoroughly - Version the manifest format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:39.336054-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:55:29.319623-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:55:29.319623-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: All 3 children closed. Created manifest system with acfs.manifest.yaml, TypeScript parser library, and installer generator producing 13 scripts.","labels":["epic","manifest"]}
